title: phalerocarpus serpyllifolia
intro: Phalerocarpus serpyllifolia, also known as the thyme-leaved sandwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant with small, oval-shaped leaves and white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Phalerocarpus serpyllifolia include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
